item.title: Example Talk
item.description:
Click on the Slides button above to view the built-in slides feature.

Slides can be added in a few ways:

  • Create slides using Wowchemy’s Slides feature and link using slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file
  • Upload an existing slide deck to static/ and link using url_slides parameter in the front matter of the talk file
  • Embed your slides (e.g. Google Slides) or presentation video on this page using shortcodes.

Further event details, including page elements such as image galleries, can be added to the body of this page.

item.title: Our paper "A Self-Supervised Learning Approach for Detecting Non-Psychotic Relapses Using Wearable-based Digital Phenotyping" is accepted at ICASSP 2024.
item.description: I am happy to announce that our paper “A Self-Supervised Learning Approach for Detecting Non-Psychotic Relapses Using Wearable-based Digital Phenotyping” got accepted at this year’s ICASSP Conference, which will be held in Seoul, South Korea from April 14 to April 19! Our paper focuses on a deep learning-based method for the early detection of non-psychotic relapses based on data from wearable devices collected from patients with non-psychotic disorders. This is a joint work with S. Eleftheriou, C. Nikou and T. Giannakopoulos conducted as part of our research in the Mutimedia Analysis Group of NCSR “Demokritos”. We will have the chance to present our work in a few months from now in Seoul.

item.title: Our group ranked 2nd in the ICASSP-featured e-Prevention Signal Processing Grand Challenge
item.description: During the past few months, me and my colleagues from the Mutimedia Analysis Group of NCSR “Demokritos” competed in the 2nd edition of the e-Prevention Signal Processing Grand Challenge. We managed to rank 2nd outof 8 research groups on the early detection of non-psychotic relapses (Track 1) based on data from wearable devices collected from patients with psychotic disorders. We are glad to announce that we will have the chance to present our paper “A Self-Supervised Learning Approach for Detecting Non-Psychotic Relapses Using Wearable-based Digital Phenotyping” at ICASSP 2024, which will be hosted in April in Seoul, South Korea.

item.title: Defense and Publication of MSc Thesis
item.description: Last week, I had the opportunity to deliver a presentation on the research conducted during the course of my MSc thesis, titled “Exploring Unimodal, Multimodal, and Few-shot Deep Learning Methods for Diagnostic Captioning.” The audience comprised members of the thesis committee, fellow students, distinguished researchers, and faculty members. This thesis was conducted under the supervision of Prof. Ion Androutsopoulos and Prof. John Pavlopoulos. The thesis committee consisted of the first supervisor, as well as Prof. George Papaioannou and Prof. Yannis Kotidis.

item.title: Distinction in the annual ImageCLEFmedical Competition
item.description: We participated in the annual ImageCLEFmedical Caption competition, where we ranked 1st in the Concept Detection and 3rd in the Caption Prediction task. This is a distinction that our group has been systematically achieving for the last few years. You can read more here.

item.title: AUEB NLP Group at ImageCLEFmedical Caption 2023
Smart Subs Subtitling App for Watching Live Virtual Dome Performances
Hybrid nanoporous materials for the separation of fluid mixtures
